matches.matched_text: finicky about Finnegan and fiddling with his faddles
matches.snippet: That's enough, genral, of finicking about Finnegan and fiddling with his faddles
matches.reasoning: Direct naming of Finnegan, the hod-carrier/bricklayer figure central to Finnegans Wake, the ballad and the wake motif

matches.matched_text: great fin may cumule! Three threeth o'er the wild!
matches.snippet: The great fin rnay cumule! Three threeth o'er the wild! Manu ware!
matches.reasoning: Direct 'fin' (likely Finn) with 'cumule' (Cumhal/cumhaill garbled), echoing mac Cumhaill/Fionn mac Cumhaill and the three-fold motif

matches.matched_text: Search ye the Finn! The sinder's under shriving sheet.
matches.snippet: Search ye the Finn! The sinder's under shriving sheet. Fa Fe Fi Fo Fum!
matches.reasoning: Explicit command 'Search ye the Finn' followed by 'Fa Fe Fi Fo Fum' thunderword pattern evoking fall/resurrection and the giant motif

matches.matched_text: Old Whitehowth he is speaking again
matches.snippet: Old Whitehowth iie is speaking again. Ope Eustace tube!
matches.reasoning: Howth is the giant's head in the Finn/HCE landscape motif of Finnegans Wake; Whitehowth echoes Whitelaw, Dublin terrain, the recumbent body
